sections.paragraph:
θυμικός, ή, όν (s. θυμός; Aristot. et al.; Philo) hot-tempered, irascible (so Aristot., Rhet. 2, 14 p. 1389a, 9; Cornutus 20 p. 39, 16; Athen. 2, 45 p. 55f; Tat. 9, 1) D 3:2.—DELG s.v. θυμός.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 461
